pattern: Fix the middle element and count how many valid left/right partners exist on each side to build ascending or descending triples in O(n^2).
hints: A brute-force triple loop is O(n^3) but n is at most 1000 — consider whether fixing one element and scanning the other two separately is cheaper.
For each index j, you only need to know: how many elements to its left are smaller (left_less) and larger (left_greater), and similarly for the right side.
For index j: ascending triples contribute left_less * right_greater; descending triples contribute left_greater * right_less. Sum over all j.

edge_inputs.description: minimum length array with no valid triple
mixed case with both ascending and descending triples
all equal values — no valid triple since ratings are unique per problem
peak in the middle reduces valid triples

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, size)
    ratings = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(ratings)
    return [ratings]
